title: eqv?/equal? on rational and complex numbers return #f for numerically identical operands
goal: Found by Sweep E edge-matrix tranche 2 (2026-07-03, master 0733dc9b), numeric_tower pairs 156-161. eqv? and equal? on RATIONAL and COMPLEX numbers return #f even when the operands are numerically identical: (eqv? (/ 1 3) (/ 1 3)) -> #f, (equal? (/ 1 3) (/ 2 6)) -> #f, (eqv? (make-rectangular 1 2) (make-rectangular 1 2)) -> #f. The `=` numeric comparator is correct ((= (/ 1 3) (/ 2 6)) -> #t) because ArithmeticCodegen::compare has the rational/complex dispatch, but eqv?/equal? fall through to identity/pointer comparison for these heap-allocated numeric types. Bignums ARE handled (value comparison), so the gap is specifically rational + complex. Reproduces identically under -r and AOT. R7RS: eqv? on two exact numbers is #t iff numerically equal; equal? on numbers is eqv?.
acceptance: edge-matrix pairs 156-161 pass and are removed from KNOWN_FAILURES
campaign_payoff: eqv?/equal? are the foundation of hash tables, member/assoc, set operations and test frameworks; silently reporting equal exact numbers as unequal corrupts any data structure keyed on rationals or complex numbers.

verifications.evidence: JIT & AOT both print '#f #f #f #f' (expected '#t #t #t #t'). = works, eqv?/equal? fall through to pointer compare for rational/complex; bignum ok.
